title: Overheard On A Saltmarsh
content:
Nymph, nymph, what are your beads?

    Green glass, goblin. Why do you stare at them?

    Give them me.

                 No.

    Give them me. Give them me.

                                No.

    Then I will howl all night in the reeds,
    Lie in the mud and howl for them.

    Goblin, why do you love them so?

    They are better than stars or water,
    Better than voices of winds that sing,
    Better than any man's fair daughter,
    Your green glass beads on a silver ring.

    Hush, I stole them out of the moon.

    Give me your beads, I want them.

                                     No.

    I will howl in a deep lagoon
    For your green glass beads, I love them so.
    Give them me. Give them.

                             No.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Harold Edward Monro delivers a powerful performance in "Overheard On A Saltmarsh"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
